content.verdict: The Edge is the cliff-clinging villa collective in Uluwatu whose eponymous pool — a glass-bottomed infinity pool cantilevered 150 meters above the ocean — became one of Instagram's most-recognizable honeymoon shots. Ten private villas, each with its own infinity pool, staff team and dedicated chef. It is, essentially, a collection of private cliff-top estates, not a hotel, and the privacy-per-dollar ratio is unmatched. Choose it for the splurge anniversary-in-honeymoon-form.
content.best_room: The Oceans Edge Villa (the flagship) gives you the glass-floor pool. If that's booked, Edge One and Edge Two are the next most dramatic, with equally cantilevered private infinity pools directly over the ocean.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival & Cliff Welcome
itinerary_7_nights.description: 45-min transfer from Denpasar to Uluwatu. Welcome by your private butler team. Villa orientation — pool, kitchen, gym, cinema. Sunset Champagne on the edge of the Edge pool. Chef-prepared dinner in the villa.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Villa Day
itinerary_7_nights.description: Slow start. Breakfast cooked by your villa chef on the deck. Morning on the cantilevered pool (photo moment). Afternoon beach shuttle to Nyang Nyang (untouched white-sand beach, 15 min). Dinner by the resident chef.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Uluwatu Temple & Surf Coast
itinerary_7_nights.description: Morning at Uluwatu Temple and Kecak fire dance at sunset. Lunch at Single Fin or Karma Beach. Stops at Padang Padang and Thomas Beach. Late dinner back at the villa.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Private Spa
itinerary_7_nights.description: In-villa couples' spa ritual — therapists come to you, full-day treatments beside your pool. Private yoga on the deck at sunset. Private fire pit dinner.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Uluwatu Beach Clubs
itinerary_7_nights.description: Full day at Ulu Cliffhouse or Omnia beach club — the Uluwatu cliff-club scene is unique. Back to the villa for a quiet dinner under stars.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Nusa Penida Day Trip
itinerary_7_nights.description: Private speedboat from Sanur or Padang Bai to Nusa Penida. Visit Kelingking 'T-Rex' beach, Broken Beach, Angel's Billabong. Picnic lunch on board. Back by sunset.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Farewell on the Edge
itinerary_7_nights.description: Final morning in the glass-bottomed pool, one more photoshoot. Late breakfast, butler-packed departure. Transfer to the airport — under an hour.

caveats: The Edge is villa-only — no common pool, no lobby, no hotel lounge. Some couples love the privacy; others miss the social atmosphere of a traditional resort.
Ocean below is dramatic but inaccessible directly — the cliff is sheer. Beach access is a shuttle ride to Nyang Nyang or nearby Uluwatu beaches.
Strong winds and exposure can mean the cantilevered pool's glass floor gets closed for safety on gusty days.

faqs.q: Do all villas have the glass-bottom pool?
faqs.a: No — the iconic glass-floor pool is only at the Oceans Edge villa. All villas have private infinity pools over the cliff, but the glass one is the signature image.
Fully private — dedicated staff, own driveway, own chef, own gate. You will not encounter other guests.
Yes, typically 3 nights, rising to 5-7 in high season (July-August, Christmas).

faqs.q: How does it compare to Alila Villas Uluwatu or Bulgari Bali?
faqs.a: Alila and Bulgari are full hotels with multiple restaurants, bars, beach clubs; The Edge is private-villa-only, more seclusion, less amenity. If you want hotel vibes choose Bulgari; if you want total privacy choose The Edge.
Yes — each villa has a dedicated chef who cooks all meals included in many packages. Menus are agreed with you daily.

faqs.q: Is the resort good for weddings?
faqs.a: Yes — cliff-top weddings at The Edge are iconic, usually intimate (10-30 guests). The cantilevered venue is booked out far in advance.

Could you please send:
